architecture.summary: SWRV is a two-stage Parser-Verifier pipeline that decomposes LLM reasoning into discrete steps. A Parser (fine-tuned compact LM) extracts problem constraints and reasoning steps from the LLM's output. A Verifier (either LM-based or symbolic solver) then validates the logical correctness of each extracted step. High-quality training annotations for the Parser are generated by a more capable LM.
trained_components: Parser (compact LM fine-tuned on annotated data)

biblio.title: LLMSR@XLLM25: SWRV: Empowering Self-Verification of Small Language Models through Step-wise Reasoning and Verification

claims.statement: SWRV framework decomposes the reasoning process into discrete inference steps and rigorously validates each one
Parser extracts problem constraints and the sequence of reasoning steps from the LLM's output
Verifier, either LM-based or powered by a symbolic solver, checks the logical correctness of every step
A compact LM is fine-tuned on a small, high-quality annotation set generated by a more capable LM to ensure robust parsing
Experiments on the LLMSR dataset built atop LogiQA show significant gains over baselines
